findings.evidence: `Rectangle.lean:53` defines `noncomputable def cIoo`, `Rectangle.lean:60/85/91/106` contain the existing basic `cIoo` lemmas, while `CyclicInterval.lean:5` imports the whole rectangle module and `CyclicInterval.lean:42-195` only states `Fin n` cyclic-interval lemmas.
findings.fix: Make `CyclicInterval.lean` the canonical home for the cyclic interval API: move `Grid.cIoo`, `mem_cIoo`, `cIoo_self`, `left_notMem_cIoo`, and `right_notMem_cIoo` out of `Rectangle.lean` into this file, then have `Rectangle.lean` import `CyclicInterval.lean`. Alternatively, put these new lemmas next to the existing `cIoo` API in `Rectangle.lean`.
findings.issue: `CyclicInterval.lean` imports `TauCeti.KnotTheory.Grid.Rectangle` even though all new declarations are generic `Fin n` facts about `Grid.cIoo`, not rectangle facts.

summary: The new lemmas are one-dimensional cyclic-interval API, but the module depends on the rectangle module that currently happens to define `Grid.cIoo`. This inverts the natural dependency and makes generic interval facts pull in rectangle theory.
